name: SliderItem
description: Draggable slider thumb primitive used inside Slider. Provides the 16px handle, slider semantics, aria value metadata, and visual states.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class for the root element

states.rationale: SliderItem uses semantic token swaps for hover, focus, pressed, and disabled states. It does not use the overlay or OKLCH state systems, so the manifest uses none for state technique classification.

keyboard.Tab: Moves focus to the enabled thumb
keyboard.Arrow keys: Parent Slider behavior should update value; SliderItem does not implement standalone value changes

accessibility.focusManagement: Enabled thumb uses tabIndex=0; disabled thumb uses tabIndex=-1

propertyMapping.note: Figma state combines visual pseudo-states with the disabled prop mapping. Only state=disabled maps to disabled={true}; normal, hover, and focus keep disabled false.

useWhen: Rendering the thumb primitive inside Slider
Demonstrating normal, hover, focus, or disabled thumb states
Showing one-thumb or two-thumb slider track compositions
doNotUseFor: Standalone user-editable range input - use Slider
Controls without an accessible name and value bounds

knownDeviations.description: No L4 color tokens in tokens.json for SliderItem color, border, or shadow states. Uses L2 semantic and shadow tokens directly. Only the radius token is component-scoped.
Figma exposes normal, hover, focus, and disabled variants. Active/pressed is code-supported but not a separate Figma variant.
SliderItem exposes slider semantics but does not implement standalone keyboard value changes. Parent Slider behavior must own value updates.
